news.headline: Bitwise launches ETF tracking corporations with 1,000+ Bitcoin, targeting 70 firms, led by MSTR, CAP at 20% to ensure diversification.
news.summary: Bitwise Asset Management, managing $12 billion in assets, has introduced the Bitwise Bitcoin Standard Corporations ETF (NYSE:OWNB) on NYSE Arca. The ETF, launched on March 11, focuses on companies holding a minimum of 1,000 Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) in their corporate treasuries. The ETF tracks the Bitwise Bitcoin Standard Corporations Index, featuring 70+ publicly traded firms that have incorporated Bitcoin to diversify their cash reserves amid economic uncertainty. The ETF prioritizes companies based on their Bitcoin holdings, with the largest holding, Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), capped at 20% to maintain diversification. Other notable holdings include MARA Holdings (NASDAQ:MSTR), CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK), Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT), Boyaa Interactive, and Metaplanet (TYO).

news.headline: Singapore Airlines partners with Salesforce for AI-powered customer service tech; will utilize Agentforce to enhance operations and develop AI solutions.
news.summary: Salesforce, Inc. (NYSE:CRM) has announced a partnership with Singapore Airlines (SIA) to improve their customer case management system. Under this agreement, SIA will integrate Agentforce, an AI-powered tool that automates tasks to optimize customer service operations. The collaboration will extend to working together at Salesforce's AI Research hub in Singapore to create AI solutions for the aviation sector. Agentforce, fueled by Salesforce's Data Cloud for streamlined data, offers personalized recommendations and solutions through AI support. Additionally, SIA will utilize Einstein's generative AI in Service Cloud for summarization purposes.

news.headline: BlackBerry QNX unveils FuSa platform for robotics w/ Intel & NexCOBOT, consolidating controls on one board for cost reduction.
news.summary: BlackBerry Limited's division QNX has unveiled a new Functional Safety (FuSa) platform tailored for advanced robotic systems. Developed in partnership with Intel and NexCOBOT, this platform combines robotic and safety controls onto a single NexCOBOT board. The aim is to assist manufacturers and developers in cutting costs, decreasing power usage, improving thermal efficiency, and reducing the chances of hardware malfunctions. This innovative platform aims to cater to the rising demand for integrated solutions in the robotics sector.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs predicts 50-100% surge in U.S. copper imports due to expected 25% tariffs, adding 200,000 to 300,000 tons to inventory by Q3. Supply deficit may lead to higher prices in 2025.
news.summary: Goldman Sachs has predicted a significant increase in U.S. copper imports due to anticipated tariffs from the Trump administration. The investment bank foresees a 25% duty on copper imports by the end of the year, prompting a rush to stockpile before the legislation is passed. This could lead to a surge of 50-100% in U.S. net copper imports, adding approximately 200,000 to 300,000 metric tons to domestic inventories by the third quarter. If this scenario unfolds, U.S. copper stockpiles could rise from 95,000 tons to potentially 400,000 tons, accounting for approximately half of global reported inventories and resulting in historically low international stock levels. Looking ahead to 2025, Goldman Sachs anticipates a global copper deficit of 180,000 tons as heightened demand for electrification, economic stimulus initiatives in China, and sluggish mine expansion take shape. The bank believes the supply-demand imbalance will be more pronounced in the latter part of the year, likely driving up prices.

news.headline: QurAlis reports phase 1 trial data for QRL-101 showing significant effects on ALS biomarkers. Brain penetration, target engagement, anti-seizure effects observed in healthy volunteers.
news.summary: The latest findings revealed significant impacts on biomarkers predicting the progression and severity of ALS, with statistical significance shown. The study also indicated evidence of brain penetration, target engagement, and potential anti-seizure effects using biomarkers for TMS-EMG and EEG. The safety and tolerability profile of the treatment was also under scrutiny during the research.

news.summary: Joseph Tsai, chairman of Alibaba Group Holding ADR (NYSE:BABA), addressed the global concern over the ongoing tariff conflict in a recent interview. Speaking at CNBC’s Converge Live event in Singapore, Tsai noted that the imposition of tariffs by the Donald Trump administration is a topic that worries many. Tsai suggested that the Trump administration may aim to increase business for American companies. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: JD Logistics Inc.'s 2024 revenue hits $25.6 billion, with 70% from external customers. Tripled profit to $1.24 billion.
news.summary: JD Logistics Inc., the logistics arm of e-commerce giant JD.com, saw a 9.7% increase in revenue in 2024, reaching 182.8 billion yuan ($25.6 billion). External customers accounted for 70% of this total, contributing 127.8 billion yuan. The company's non-IFRS annual profit also surged to 7.92 billion yuan, almost three times higher than the previous year's 2.76 billion yuan. This significant growth was attributed to enhanced efficiencies from technology upgrades in its integrated supply chain solutions, with the non-IFRS margin climbing from 1.7% in 2023 to 4.3% in 2024. JD Logistics Inc. emphasized diversifying its customer base and optimizing operational efficiencies, resulting in record profits and margins for the company.

news.headline: Global emergency medical service products market valued at USD 31 billion in 2024, expected to grow at 6.4% CAGR, reaching USD 62 billion by 2035.
news.summary: The global emergency medical service products market was valued at USD 31 billion in 2024 and is expected to reach USD 62 billion by 2035, with a projected CAGR of 6.4%. The growth is driven by the increasing demand for efficient healthcare solutions during emergencies, with advanced medical devices like monitoring systems, defibrillators, and communication tools playing a key role. The emphasis on rapid response and patient safety is boosting market expansion, leading to a demand for disinfectants and cleaning agents for infection control. North America is forecasted to hold a 6% market share in 2035, with a significant absolute $ opportunity of USD 28.71 billion between 2025 to 2035. Technology integration like real-time data sharing and telemedicine is enhancing the effectiveness of emergency medical service products.

news.headline: Endo to present Post hoc data analysis of Phase 3 trial for Peyronie's disease with XIAFLEX at Urological Assoc. meeting.
news.summary: Today, in Malvern, Pennsylvania, Endo, Inc. revealed that they will present findings from a post hoc analysis of a Phase 3 clinical trial of XIAFLEX® (CCH) for Peyronie's disease patients at the 89th annual meeting. The study examined the effectiveness of the treatment in addressing PD. XIAFLEX® is a collagenase clostridium histolyticum drug. The Phase 3 trial involved a total of test subjects and aimed to assess the drug's impact on the condition. The results of this analysis will offer valuable insights into the potential of XIAFLEX® as a treatment for Peyronie's disease.Character count: 1011

news.headline: Investors shifting focus to sustainable Web3 gaming, avoiding hype-driven projects post layoffs; selective flow of capital.
news.summary: The Web3 gaming industry is experiencing tighter investment conditions as investors prioritize sustainable projects over hype-driven fundraisers. Gunzilla Games Web3 director noted that blockchain gaming is like a "musical chairs" game with no new money coming in and users moving on to extract value from different projects. Illuvium and Sky Mavis both announced layoffs, showcasing the need to operate with leaner teams in the current market. Despite this, professionals in the industry stated that capital is still present but investors are being more selective. Investors are now focused on projects that integrate Web3 elements to enhance player experience rather than solely relying on financial incentives. Some successful projects like Fableborne have still attracted significant capital, showing that fresh money is still flowing in. The days of easily raising funds by adding NFTs to a game are over, and investors are now looking for strong fundamentals and sustainable economies. Building great games and understanding the distribution process are now key elements to attract Web3 gaming capital. Overall, the industry is facing challenges but there are opportunities for projects that focus on delivering compelling content and gameplay, sustainable user acquisition, building a strong in-app economy, and cultivating a loyal user base.
